title: DNA Separation and Detection on Agarose Gels
author: LI-COR Biosciences
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.q92dz8e

steps.title: Prepare desired agarose (0
steps.instruction: Prepare desired agarose (0.8%, 1.0%, 1.2%, etc.) in 1X TAE or 1X TBE buffer.

steps.title: Cool solution until warm to the...
steps.instruction: Cool solution until warm to the touch (60°F) prior to pouring in casting tray.

steps.title: Pour molten agarose solution into casting...
steps.instruction: Pour molten agarose solution into casting tray and set comb into place. Allow gel to solidify.

steps.title: Remove comb and place gel in buffer tank
steps.instruction: Remove comb and place gel in buffer tank.

steps.title: Prepare enough buffer (1X TAE or...
steps.instruction: Prepare enough buffer (1X TAE or 1X TBE) to fill the apparatus.

steps.title: Prepare samples with loading buffer and load in gel
steps.instruction: Prepare samples with loading buffer and load in gel.

steps.title: Prepare enough solution of 1X TAE...
steps.instruction: Prepare enough solution of 1X TAE or water to cover the agarose gel. • Ethidium Bromide — Add 5 µL of 10 mg/mL EtBr stock solution to 100 mL of buffer (final concentration: 0.5 µg/mL). • SYBR Safe — Add 10 µL of 10,000X SYBR Safe stock solution to 100 mL of buffer (final concentration:1X).

steps.title: Soak gel for 20 minutes in the prepared solution
steps.instruction: Soak gel for 20 minutes in the prepared solution. 00:20:00, Soak

steps.title: Destain in water for 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Destain in water for 20 minutes. Repeat as necessary to remove background from the gel. 00:20:00, Destain

steps.title: Image on the Odyssey Fc Imager using the 600 channel
steps.instruction: Image on the Odyssey Fc Imager using the 600 channel. Refer to the Image Acquisition on the Odyssey Fc Imager protocol for more information.
